fundamental_rights: Article 21A — Right to Free and Compulsory Education (children 6-14 years)
Article 15(3) — State may make special provisions for children (reservation in education)
Article 29-30 — Cultural and Educational Rights (minority institutions)
Article 14 — Right to Equality (no arbitrary discrimination in admissions)

laws.name: Right to Education Act, 2009 (RTE)
sections: Section 3 (free and compulsory education up to age 14)
Section 12 (25% seats in private schools for EWS/disadvantaged)
Section 17 (prohibition of physical punishment and mental harassment)
laws.summary: Every child aged 6-14 has a right to free and compulsory education. Private schools must reserve 25% seats for EWS children.
laws.remedy: Complaint to District Education Officer; appeal to State Commission for Protection of Child Rights

laws.name: UGC Act, 1956 and UGC Regulations
sections: UGC (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal of Sexual Harassment) Regulations, 2015
laws.summary: UGC regulates universities and colleges. Anti-ragging is mandatory. Every institution must have an ICC for sexual harassment.
laws.remedy: Complaint to UGC at ugc.ac.in; call UGC anti-ragging helpline 1800-180-5522

laws.name: Prohibition of Ragging in Educational Institutions
sections: Supreme Court Guidelines in UoI v. Raghavan 2009
laws.summary: Ragging is a criminal and civil offence. Institutions must expel ragging perpetrators. Mandatory anti-ragging committee.
laws.remedy: Call anti-ragging helpline: 1800-180-5522; file complaint at antiragging.in; FIR at police station

laws.name: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) / IPC
sections: Section 115 BNS (voluntarily causing hurt — for physical ragging)
laws.summary: Ragging involving physical assault or threats can result in criminal prosecution.
laws.remedy: File FIR at nearest police station

laws.name: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989
laws.summary: Caste-based discrimination in educational institutions is a criminal offence.
laws.remedy: File complaint with police; approach SC/ST Commission

steps: Document all incidents: dates, witnesses, emails, notice board photos
For ragging: call anti-ragging helpline 1800-180-5522 (24x7) or file at antiragging.in
For fee disputes: write to the institution's grievance officer first (get acknowledgement)
If unresolved: approach the University's Grievance Cell, then UGC/AICTE/State Education Department
For RTE violations: complain to District Education Officer or State Commission for Protection of Child Rights
For caste-based discrimination: file complaint with SC/ST Commission and local police
For sexual harassment: file complaint with Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) within 3 months
For free legal aid: NALSA 15100 or District Legal Services Authority
